prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like assembling a stained-glass window in the dark—your hands fumble for the right colored shards (vocabulary), each piece clicks uncertainly into place (grammar), and only later, when enough fragments align, does a sliver of light reveal the emerging picture. Unlike children who build their windows in daylight, intuitively matching hues, you work by memory, logic, and stubborn guesswork, often cutting your fingers on sharp edges of mispronunciation or misunderstood idioms. Yet when dawn finally breaks through your mosaic, the view it frames of another culture’s soul feels all the more luminous for the struggle it took to create.
